steps.title: Identify Salman-Engin's key finding
steps.content: Text 1 explicitly frames their research as departing from typical studies: while prior research focused on ongoing relationships, Salman-Engin and colleagues demonstrated that brief interactions with strangers (a shuttle driver) produce positive effects on well-being.

steps.title: Recognize what the model overlooks
steps.content:
The model in Text 2 organizes all connections as existing social network ties of varying closeness.

Salman-Engin would note that this framework highlights a research gap: stranger interactions, which their study showed to be beneficial, are entirely absent from the model.

Answer A states that the model underscores research's failure to capture this beneficial connection category.

The other answers misidentify the issue:
  • B concerns longevity (not Salman-Engin's focus)
  • C reverses their findings (they found strangers positive)
  • D addresses distinctions among close relationships rather than strangers outside the network
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
